note: HISTORICAL. This verdict captures the initial slot-coords refactor only. The same session shipped three follow-on waves on top — legacy-strip, path-fix, and tetromino bag shapes. AC8's legacy-fallback PASS was reverted by the legacy-strip wave. Current contract lives in docs/bag-item-placement-persistence.md. Current test total is 311/311 unit+scenario (+13 bag-shape, +2 use-shop) with 17/17 bag-relevant Playwright e2e.
summary: Bagged-item storage migrated from virtual grid coords to slot coords; bag_id tightened from artifactId to loadout row id. Full 297-test suite green at ship time.

AC1.proof: tests/game/bag-items.test.js [Req 12-D, 5-A] asserts round1Bagged.y === 0 (slot coord, not INVENTORY_ROWS virtual coord) after PUT.

AC2.proof: Same scenario asserts round1Bagged.bag_id === round1BagRowId (SELECTed from the bag's own row), not the 'moss_pouch' artifact id.

AC3.proof: Same scenario asserts round2BagRowId !== round1BagRowId and zero orphan rows; copyRoundForward two-pass map implementation in app/server/services/game-run-loadout.js. Later strengthened by the legacy-strip wave to throw on dangling bag_id references instead of propagating them.

AC4.proof: tests/game/validator-split.test.js — slot-bounds rejection, per-bag overlap rejection, duplicate-bag row-id disambiguation. Extended by the tetromino-shapes wave with shape-mask slot-cell enforcement.

AC5.proof: web/src/composables/useGameRun.js:buildLoadoutPayloadItems emits y: item.y - info.startRow and bagId: info.bagRowId. End-to-end exercised by the AC1/AC2 scenario.

AC6.proof: tests/web/loadout-projection.test.js — slot-to-virtual reconstruction (single, second-slot, multi-bag, duplicate-bag). The legacy-fallback cases cited at ship time (bagId=artifactId, (-1,-1)) were removed in the legacy-strip wave; the inactive-bag and OOB fallbacks remain as defensive runtime guards.

AC7.proof: tests/web/use-shop.test.js [bag-relayout] — deactivate earlier empty bag shifts later bag's items up; rotate earlier empty bag shifts later bag's items by rowCount delta; deactivate non-empty bag stays blocked.

AC8.proof: Shipped with the projection fallback absorbing legacy-format rows. Subsequently reverted by the legacy-strip wave (the app never reached production so no legacy data exists). Current contract: the validator throws on missing bag row ids, and the projection routes only inactive-bag / OOB cases to the container. See tests/game/validator-split.test.js 'rejects bagId that is an artifactId rather than a row id' and 'rejects a bag row missing its loadout row id'.

out_of_scope: Playwright round-2 prep screenshot — DB + projection unit tests cover the invariants; dev stack not spun up for this task. (Subsequently covered by the full Playwright bag-relevant suite once the path-fix wave landed.)
SQL backfill of legacy rows — explicit non-goal, pre-production data only, projection fallback absorbs legacy shapes. (Subsequently strengthened to loud rejection by the legacy-strip wave.)
